commit.message:
Fix context menus rendering behind floating sidebar toolbar (#580)

## Problem
Context menus (session right-click, group `⋯` menu, header `⋯` overflow)
render **behind** the floating sidebar toolbar buttons (New, Manual
sort, refresh, filter chips).

## Root Cause
Multiple positioned elements with explicit `z-index` values create
**stacking contexts** that trap descendant `position: fixed` menus —
even though the menus have `z-index: 10000`, they can't escape a parent
stacking context with a lower z-index.

## Fix
- **Session/group context menus:** Add `has-open-menu` CSS class to
`sidebar-content` when any menu is open. Collapse stacking contexts on
toolbar elements (`sidebar-top-tools`, `sidebar-compose-panel`,
`sidebar-organize-shell-compact`, `sidebar-support-fab`) to `z-index:
auto` — buttons stay visible but the menu paints on top.
- **Header overflow menu:** Use `:has()` to bump `.sidebar-header` from
z-index 3 → 50 when the overflow is hovered/focused.
- **Cleanup:** Remove unnecessary `z-index: 2` from `.session-actions`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add deflate Content-Encoding decompression support

When an upstream sends Content-Encoding: deflate (despite our Accept-Encoding
request for gzip/identity), the proxy now decompresses the body, applies string
replacements, and strips the Content-Encoding header — same as for gzip.

HTTP 'deflate' is ambiguous: some servers send zlib-wrapped DEFLATE (RFC 1950,
0x78 magic byte) while others send raw DEFLATE (RFC 1951). Try zlib first;
fall back to raw flate if the first two bytes aren't the zlib header (0x78 xx).

Also fix: CSP ws:// scheme was incorrectly mapped to http:// instead of ws://
(ws:// fell through to the switch default). Now both wss:// and ws:// map to
ws:// correctly.

Tests added:
- TestDeflateResponseBodyRewrite: zlib-wrapped + raw-deflate subtests
- CSP ws-scheme test case already committed separately

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix export aspect ratio to match preview

The export was using a fixed 1920x1080 target resolution from
GetResolutionDimensions(), which stretched non-16:9 recordings.

commit.message:
feat: campaigns trigger on score decline, not just rejection streaks

Campaign triggers are now Gate rules:
  reliabilityCampaignRule = Gate.or(consecutiveRejects >= N, reliabilityDeclining(2))
  modularityCampaignRule  = Gate.or(consecutiveRejects >= N, modularityDeclining(2))
  radicalPlanRule         = consecutiveRejects >= N (only on streaks)

Each campaign fires independently when its rule returns SUCCESS:
- Reliability declining 2+ iterations → reliability campaign
- Modularity declining 2+ iterations → modularity campaign
- Both declining → both campaigns run
- Rejection streak → all three (campaigns + radical plan)

This catches divergence BEFORE the terminator hits Rule 3 (stop).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- ExtendFootprintTtl: readWrite must be empty, readOnly must contain only
  Soroban entries (ExtendFootprintTTLOpFrame.cpp:321-370)

Added is_soroban_entry() and is_persistent_entry() helpers matching
stellar-core's LedgerTypeUtils.h definitions.

Closes #1490
Closes #1488

commit.message:
Fix 2x speed export: unify video FPS with compositor FPS

The export was producing a 60fps video but the compositor only had 30
frames per second of cursor/click data (capped to match the preview).
This caused the video to play at 2x speed with click animations lagging
behind the visuals.

Fix: the export video FPS is now forced to match the compositor FPS
(30fps, same as the editor preview). Both the MediaStreamSource frame
rate and the encoding profile use the same value, so the exported video
plays at exactly the same speed as the preview.

Remove `utils/math_util.rs` from `diskann-providers` (#921)

`math_util.rs` in `diskann-providers` was a grab-bag module containing
unused utilities and a vector generation helper. This PR removes the
file by deleting unused functions and replacing the remaining usage with
the equivalent from `diskann-utils`.

## Changes

- **Deleted unused functions** from
`diskann-providers/src/utils/math_util.rs`:
  - `process_residuals` — no callers
  - `convert_usize_to_u64` — no callers

- **Replaced `generate_vectors_with_norm`** in
`diskann-tools/src/utils/random_data_generator.rs` with
`f32::with_approximate_norm()` from `diskann-utils`.

- **Replaced `generate_vectors_with_norm`** in the `diskann_async.rs`
sphere tests with inline Gaussian sphere sampling (using
`rand_distr::StandardNormal`) and a local `CastSphericalF32` helper
trait. This preserves the original Gaussian distribution required for
Cosine-metric ANN tests to pass — `WithApproximateNorm` uses uniform
sampling, which caused test failures.

- **Deleted `diskann-providers/src/utils/math_util.rs`** entirely.

- **Updated `diskann-providers/src/utils/mod.rs`**: removed the `pub mod
math_util` declaration and its re-exports.

- **Merged `origin/main`**: resolved a rename-collision conflict where
`main` moved `diskann-providers/src/utils/math_util.rs` →
`diskann-disk/src/utils/math_util.rs` while this branch had already
deleted it from `diskann-providers`.

Re-enable MBO order conflation in write loop

Now that the root cause of book drift is fixed (snapshot packets no longer
processed during RealTime), conflation can be safely restored. The state
machine correctly coalesces orders within a drain cycle:
  Add+Delete → cancel, Add+Update → Add(new), Update+Update → Update(new),
  Update+Delete → Delete, Delete+Add → Update(new)

Book clears purge pending orders for the affected security/side before
being serialized. This reduces WS bandwidth significantly.

Add --refresh flag to ADIF import for updating existing records (#99)

When refresh mode is active and a duplicate is detected, the engine
updates the existing record instead of skipping it. Non-empty import
fields overwrite, but identity metadata (local_id, created_at,
sync_status, QSL fields) and fields absent from the import are
preserved. This enables re-importing a corrected ADIF file to fix
values (e.g. RST) without losing enrichment data.

Proto: add bool refresh to ImportAdifRequest, uint32 records_updated
to ImportAdifResponse.

Engine: refactor is_duplicate_import to find_duplicate_import,
add merge_qso_for_refresh, track records_updated in
AdifImportSummary.

CLI: add --refresh flag, display Updated count in output.

Includes four new integration tests covering refresh updates,
field preservation, mixed new/existing, and the unchanged skip
behavior without refresh.

Fixes #85
